meta.name: Chilaquiles Rojos
meta.subtitle: Crispy tortilla chips quickly tossed in warm salsa roja, topped with eggs and fresh garnishes

summary: Classic chilaquiles rojos built for SSA planning: a fast salsa roja (or use jarred), crisp tortilla chips, and flexible toppings (eggs, cheese, beans, chicken). The key is controlling softness: quick toss for crunchy, longer simmer for tender.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a hearty breakfast/brunch; increase chips + sauce for larger crowds.

ingredients.label: Corn tortillas
ingredients.notes: Stale tortillas make the best chips

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
ingredients.notes: For frying or baking chips + sautéing

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Divide across chips + sauce + final seasoning

ingredients.label: Roma tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Or substitute 2 cups canned crushed tomatoes

ingredients.label: Jalapeño
ingredients.notes: Use half for mild; remove seeds for less heat

ingredients.label: Chicken broth (or water)
ingredients.notes: Adjust thickness; vegetable broth works too

steps.title: Make tortilla chips (or use store-bought)
steps.body: Cut tortillas into wedges. Option A (quick fry): heat 2–3 tbsp oil in a skillet and fry chips in batches until crisp; drain and lightly salt. Option B (bake): toss wedges with a little oil and bake at 400°F (205°C) 10–12 minutes, flipping once. Set aside.

steps.title: Cook and blend salsa roja
steps.body: In a saucepan, simmer tomatoes with half the onion, garlic, and jalapeño (optional) with a splash of water until softened, 6–8 minutes. Blend until smooth with broth, cumin/oregano (optional), and chipotle (optional). Simmer 2–4 minutes to thicken; season with salt and a squeeze of lime if desired.
checks: Sauce is warm and slightly thick (coats chips without becoming soup)

steps.title: Cook eggs and prep toppings
steps.body: Cook eggs to preference (fried over-easy, scrambled, etc.). Prep toppings: crumble cheese, chop cilantro, slice avocado, warm beans/chicken if using, and dice remaining onion for garnish.

steps.title: Toss chips with salsa (control crunch)
steps.body: In a wide skillet, warm salsa over medium-low. Add chips and toss just 30–60 seconds for crunchy chilaquiles, or 2–3 minutes for softer chilaquiles. Remove from heat immediately when texture is right.
checks: Chips are coated; texture matches preference

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Plate chilaquiles and top with eggs, cheese, crema, cilantro, onion, avocado, and optional beans/chicken. Serve immediately.

variations.label: Chilaquiles Verdes
variations.notes: Swap salsa roja for salsa verde (tomatillo-based). Great with crema and chicken.

variations.label: Fast Mode (Jarred Salsa)
variations.notes: Use 2–2.5 cups good jarred salsa. Warm and simmer 2–3 minutes to thicken; proceed with tossing.

variations.label: Chicken Chilaquiles
variations.notes: Add warm shredded chicken on top or toss into the chilaquiles right after chips for a heartier plate.

variations.label: Dairy-Free
variations.notes: Skip cheese/crema; add extra avocado and a squeeze of lime for richness and balance.

storage.refrigeration: Store salsa separately for up to 4 days. Chips are best fresh; store-bought chips keep well.
storage.freezing: Freeze salsa only (up to 2 months). Do not freeze assembled chilaquiles.
storage.reheat: Reheat salsa on the stove; toss fresh chips just before serving.

notes: Serve immediately after tossing for best texture and food safety.
If using eggs, keep hot foods hot and refrigerate leftovers promptly.

prepNotes: Make salsa ahead and reheat for fast assembly.
Keep chips separate until the last minute to control softness.
planner.leftoversPlan: Save extra salsa + toppings; make a fresh batch of chips next time.

notes: Key control knob is texture: short toss = crunchy; longer toss = tender.
For a deeper red flavor, add a small toasted dried chile (guajillo) to the salsa blend.
If your tortillas are very fresh and soft, bake chips rather than fry for better crunch control.
